text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-hook-form repository at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: a subscription created via `form.subscribe({ formState: { ... }, callback })` receives a payload that includes the `name` of the field involved in the event that triggered it. If you call `clearErrors('firstName')` and then, later, trigger an unrelated form-state update that has no field name of its own (for example submitting the form via `handleSubmit`), the subscriber's callback fires with `name` still set to `'firstName'` — a stale value left over from the earlier `clearErrors` call — instead of `undefined`. In other words, the per-event metadata (`name`/`type`) from one notification is being persisted into form state and bleeding into later, unrelated notifications. Fix the library source so that per-event metadata is not persisted across updates and each subscriber notification reports only the name (if any) of the event that actually triggered it. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: dependencies are already installed (via `npm install -g pnpm@10.34.5 && CYPRESS_INSTALL_BINARY=0 pnpm install --no-frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts`). Run tests with `node_modules/.bin/jest --config ./scripts/jest/jest.config.js src/__tests__/useForm/subscribe.test.tsx`.

text: I’ll trace how `form.subscribe` builds and dispatches its payload, then add a focused source fix and run the specified subscription test file. I’ll preserve any existing worktree changes and verify the stale metadata scenario directly.

The likely fault is in `createFormControl`: event fields are merged into `_formState` in some notification paths, while subscribers later spread that state into their payload. I’m reading those paths and the existing subscribe tests now to identify the smallest correction and avoid changing unrelated form-state behavior.

text: The reproduction mechanism is confirmed: `clearErrors` emits `{ name: inputName, ... }`; `shouldRenderFormState` calls `_setFormState`, and `_setFormState` currently merges every emitted key into `_formState`, including `name` and `type`. That makes later emissions that omit those keys inherit the old metadata. I’m checking the helper’s contract and whether any internal caller relies on storing those event-only fields before editing.

The helper confirms the intended boundary: `name` and `type` are event metadata, while `_formState` should contain only persistent form state. I’ll make `_setFormState` discard both keys before merging; notification callbacks still receive the current event object because `_subscribe` spreads `formState` afterward. This also covers `clearErrors`, `setError`, field changes, and any future event emitter consistently.

text: The first fix passes the requested suite, but a broader audit found one direct merge in the validation-error path that also writes `name` into `_formState`, bypassing `_setFormState`. I’m removing that second persistence route so the invariant holds for all event metadata, not just subscriber-driven updates.
